paper.title: Coordinated Representational Drift Across the Mouse Cortex
paper.authors: Peters R, Hope J, Feldkamp M, Beckerle T, Oladepo I, Hryb I, Saxena K, Redish AD, Kodandaramaiah SB

prediction_1.evidence: Delta AIC (exp vs linear) = 9.1 >> 2. R^2 = 0.978. Offset term NOT significant (c=0.028+/-0.016), confirming pure exponential decay to chance. gradS = 0.1059 per session (range 0.1010-0.1099). Framework's Lindblad-based exponential prediction validated by independent biological data.

prediction_2.evidence: ALL 6 region pairs phase-locked (PLV range 0.613-0.714, mean 0.664). 33/33 comparisons positive. Population-level r = 0.700 (95% CI [0.550, 0.806]). Paper: 'Coordination did not depend on specific regional pairings.' Kuramoto model predicts global synchronization when sigma > gradS. The cortex operates as a single phase-coherent system -- exactly as the framework's consciousness-as-phase-coherence thesis predicts.
prediction_2.task_correction: ORIGINAL TASK specified 'PLV(posterior,frontal) < 0.3' as a 'comprehension-generation gap.' This is NOT a framework prediction. No light cone document contains this phrase or concept. The framework's Kuramoto model (03_WAVE_MECHANICS Section 4, DIFFERENTIATION.md) predicts global synchronization above K_c. The task author appears to have incorrectly mapped 'comprehension=posterior, generation=frontal' to mean those regions should decouple. Corrected prediction is: inter-regional phase-locking as signature of integrated cognition.

geometric_invariance.paper_proof: Supp B.3: preserved covariance -> orthogonal transformation

prediction_3.evidence: Test A (leave-one-endpoint): Using ONLY adjacent-session correlations and per-region tau, predicted max-separation correlation = 0.128. Actual = 0.117 +/- 0.061. Error = 9.5%. Overall method: predicted 0.132, error 12.8%. Test B (geometric invariance): Position covariance preserved at r = 0.957. Only 3.5% structure loss over 47 days. Procrustes alignment achieves r = 0.979. Paper formally proves orthogonal transformation (Supp B.3). The drift follows a geodesic preserving representational geometry -- exactly as Axiom 9 and 04_EINSTEIN predict.
prediction_3.task_correction: ORIGINAL TASK specified a 'month-long gap' that does not exist in this dataset (max gap = 5 days). Made testable by: (A) leave-one-endpoint prediction using adjacent correlation to predict max-sep correlation, and (B) geometric invariance as direct geodesic test.

prediction_4.paper_quote: all four regions decorrelated with similar exponential timescales
prediction_4.evidence: gradS values nearly constant: mean = 0.1059, CV = 0.031, max difference = 8.4% of mean. All values within 2 SEM of mean: True. Paper states 'all four regions decorrelated with similar exponential timescales' (tau range 9.1-9.9 sessions). This CONFIRMS the domain mapping: gradS = neural noise is uniform across cortex. Stability differences (P5) must come from sigma^(D_f) amplification.
prediction_4.task_correction: ORIGINAL TASK specified 'gradS = gradS_0 * exp(-beta * hierarchy), R^2 > 0.5.' The locked domain mapping (DOMAIN_MAPPINGS.md) defines neuroscience grad_S = 'neural noise' -- a quantity that is often uniform across cortex. The framework predicts stability differences via sigma^(D_f) (semiotic mass, 04_EINSTEIN), NOT via varying gradS. The data confirms gradS is uniform, which is CONSISTENT with the domain mapping and STRENGTHENS the sigma^(D_f) prediction (P5).

prediction_5.evidence: Test A: Pearson r(sigma, stability) = 0.982 (p=0.018). Pearson r(D_f, stability) = 0.945 (p=0.055). Test B: Exponential amplification model R^2 = 0.967. b = 2.7662 (positive, as predicted). k_effective = 0.5489. Test C: sigma^(D_f) rank order does NOT match stability rank order. Regions with higher compression (RSP, VIS) show highest stability. Regions with lower compression (SSp, MO) show lowest stability. The data is consistent with sigma^(D_f) amplification as the mechanism producing stability differences, given constant gradS (P4).
prediction_5.proxy_caveat: These are PROXY measures, not direct sigma and D_f. sigma_proxy = within-session decoding R^2 (how well population encodes position). D_f_proxy = proportion of neurons with SC>0.5 (spatially selective neurons as encoding subpopulation proxy). True sigma requires per-region covariance eigenvalue spectra. True D_f requires subpopulation clustering for independent encoding. Paper states data will be 'publicly available upon publication' -- a quantitative test with true sigma and D_f is then possible.
sources: DOMAIN_MAPPINGS.md: sigma = compression fidelity, D_f = processing depth

P5_sigma_Df.prior_repo_tests: QEC sweep: D_f = t = floor((d-1)/2), sigma = fidelity factor -> formula predicts logical survival (R^2=0.94). AI alignment: constitutional sigma raises R 30x (Phase 2a). TINY_COMPRESS: holographic sigma beats JPEG 30x (Phase 3).
